metadata.title:
CREMATORY TO
RUN LIGHT


PLANT

Wasted Heat Can be Utli-
zed to Generate Steam


Power.

pages.raw_text:
There is a possibility that a
municipal lighting plant which
City Engineer Ralston has been
working on for some time may be
run by steam furnished by the Gre
matory, instead of by water power,
as first suggested. There is plenty
room for an electric plant on the
crematory grounds and unlimited
heat now going to waste.


Councilman Funk and Superin-
tendent Merritt of the crematory
met yesterday and discussed the
matter Mr.. Merritt is of the opin-
ion the problems can be solved
with little study and stated that he
would give his time to it.


At present the intense heat gen.
Grated at the crematory might as
well be making steam for other
purposes, one of them for the pur-
pose of producing sufficient power
to run an electric plant. Mr.. Mer
rift grasps the possibilities of the
suggestion and is certain the Gre
matory may be put to this other
use besides merely incinerating
garbage, etc.


Although municipal plant prob
ably would not reduce the expense
of arc light 10 any great extent,
it would materially reduce the ex-
+. pense of incandescents to the small
home owner.


Mr.. Funk refers to Seattle as a
demonstration of the saving posi-
BILE with public plant. In this city
the charge is Is cents kilowat
hour; in Seattle, the municipal
plant charge is Sh cents.


Before the year is out the city
will probably be paying the w. w.
p. $4S year FOR 1000 lights. On
this bases alone, allowing for a
reasonable prGit which the power
trust must make from its city con-
tract, it would be but a matter of
a few years when the probable
100000 cost of a municipal plant
would be paid for.
